title: Module 1
content:
Introduction– environmental legislation and regulation-water quality
standards, Sources and classification of wastewater- Types of water
pollutants and their effects, Wastewater sampling-preservation and analysis.
Physical and chemical characteristics of wastewater-determination of solids
and organic matter, dissolved oxygen (Oxygen Sag Curve), biochemical
oxygen demand, chemical oxygen demand.
Pretreatment and primary treatment of wastewater (brief overview- diagram,
principle and working of equipment only, design not needed) - Screening, grit
removal, oil removal and equalization, neutralization, coagulation, flocculation
and sedimentation, clarifiers and clariflocculation.

title: Module 2
content:
Secondary wastewater treatment methods(brief overview- diagram,
principle and working of equipment only, Modelling and design not needed)
– Aerobic biological processes - activated sludge process, trickling filters,
oxidation ditch, aeration lagoon, rotating biological contactors, aerobic
fluidized bed bioreactors. Anaerobic digestion process - anaerobic filter,
anaerobic contact process, anaerobic fluidized bed bioreactors, up flow
anaerobic  sludge  blanket  (UASB).  Tertiary  treatment-  Disinfections:
Chlorination and Ozonation – Adsorption sand filters.

title: Module 3
content:
Sludge treatment and disposal - sludge thickening, sludge conditioning,
sludge dewatering, sludge digestion and composting (brief overview).
Solid waste treatment-sources and classification, collection and disposal
methods, open dumping, sanitary landfill, incineration, composting.
Hazardous waste -types of hazardous waste, health effects, treatment and
disposal methods.
Noise pollution - effects of noise on people, Noise control methods.

title: Module 4
content:
Air pollution: Units of measurement, sources, classification. Sampling and
analysis of air pollutants, Effects of air pollution-global warming and ozone
depletion. Meteorological phenomena on air quality-lapse rates, pressure,
wind, moisture and dispersion.
Air pollution control methods and equipment (brief overview- diagram,
principle and working of equipment only, design not needed) - Control devices
for particulate contaminants: Gravitational settling, Centrifugal Collectors,
Wet Collectors, Fabric filters and Electrostatic Precipitators (ESP). Control
devices for gaseous contaminants: Adsorption, Absorption, Condensation,
Combustion, Automobile Emission Control.
Suggestion on Project Topics
1. Collect sample from any polluted water source or effluent from nearby industry like food
processing, metal polishing, pulp and paper etc., analyze the physical, chemical and biological
characteristics and suggest a treatment process with required primary, secondary and tertiary
equipment to treat the effluent according to the disposable standards specified by legislation.
Use modelling and simulation software to design the process.
2. Collect air sample from flue gas, urban polluted area, analyze air quality index and design
suitable air quality monitoring/control equipment.
3. Collect any solid waste sample (like e-waste, hazardous waste, MSW), analyze the
characteristics and suggest/design treatment process/equipment.
OR
Divide the project into 3 parts as follows Project I
(15 marks): Do any one project from the given list
1. Identify socially relevant / environmental issue in your nearby locality, explore it and
suggest remedial measures.
2. Identify a waste water source in nearby locality and study the physical, chemical
characteristics.
3. Collect air sample from flue gas, urban polluted area, analyze air quality index.
4. Collect any solid waste sample (like e-waste, hazardous waste, MSW), analyze the
characteristics.
Project II (10 marks): Do any one project from the given list
1. Design grit chamber and sedimentation tank for a specific wastewater pollution problem.
2. Design an activated sludge reactor for a specified wastewater pollution issue.
3. Design trickling filter and rotating biological contactor separately to address a specific
wastewater pollution challenge.
4. Design any one anaerobic reactor for waste water treatment.
5. Design of an anaerobic digester for a given solid waste management issue.
6. Design any two air pollution controlling equipment.
Project III (5 marks):
1. Design of any single pollution controlling equipment for a specific problem using
software like DWSIM, BIOWIN, SUMO, etc. or excel.
